questions.q: In whose kingdom did the Pandavas spend their thirteenth year of exile?
options: King Drupada's Panchala
King Shalya's Madra
King Virata's Matsya
King Jarasandha's Magadha
questions.explanation: The Pandavas hid for the thirteenth year as servants in the Matsya kingdom of King Virata.

questions.q: What name did Arjuna take during the year in hiding?
options: Brihannala
Granthika
Kanka
Vallabha
questions.explanation: Arjuna lived as Brihannala, the dance and music teacher of Virata's inner palace.

questions.q: What work did Bhima perform in Virata's palace during the hidden year?
options: Stable master for the king's horses
Cook in the royal kitchens
Guard at the palace gates
Keeper of the royal treasury
questions.explanation: Bhima served as the cook Vallabha, also entertaining the court as a wrestler.

questions.q: Under what identity did Draupadi live during the hidden year?
options: As a priestess in the city temple
As a weaver in the royal workshops
As a nurse to the royal children
As Sairandhri, the queen's maid
questions.explanation: Draupadi served Queen Sudeshna as the hairdresser and maid called Sairandhri.

questions.q: What name and role did Yudhishthira assume at Virata's court?
options: Tantipala, the chief cowherd
Granthika, the keeper of horses
Kanka, a brahmin courtier and dice companion
Vallabha, the cook of the royal kitchens
questions.explanation: Yudhishthira became Kanka, a brahmin who entertained King Virata with dice and counsel.

questions.q: Where did the Pandavas hide their weapons for the year of disguise?
options: Wrapped like a corpse and hung in a shami tree
Buried beneath the floor of a temple
Sunk in a sealed chest in the river
Walled up in a cave outside the city
questions.explanation: They bundled the weapons to look like a corpse and hung them in a shami tree near the burning ground so no one would touch them.

questions.q: What was the penalty if the Pandavas were discovered during the thirteenth year?
options: Immediate execution by the Kauravas
The entire term of exile would have to be served again
Permanent banishment from the land of Bharata
Surrender of their weapons to Duryodhana forever
questions.explanation: By the terms of the dice game, discovery in the thirteenth year meant repeating the whole sentence of exile.

questions.q: What did Brihannala teach in Virata's palace?
options: Archery to the king's soldiers
Scripture to the royal priests
Statecraft to the king's heir
Dance and music to princess Uttara
questions.explanation: Brihannala taught dancing, singing, and music to princess Uttara and her companions in the inner apartments.

questions.q: Which Pandava worked as Granthika, the keeper of Virata's horses?
options: Sahadeva
Bhima
Nakula
Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: Nakula, a famed master of horses, tended the royal stables under the name Granthika.

questions.q: What duty did Sahadeva take up during the hidden year?
options: Tending the king's cattle as Tantipala
Serving wine at the king's table
Keeping accounts of the royal stores
Training hounds for the royal hunt
questions.explanation: Sahadeva became Tantipala, the overseer of King Virata's vast cattle herds.

questions.q: Why was Arjuna able to live convincingly as Brihannala?
options: He used an illusion taught by the gandharva Chitrasena
Urvashi's curse of unmanliness, limited by Indra to one chosen year, took effect
He had trained as a dancer in Indra's court for five years
Krishna's maya veiled his true form from every eye
questions.explanation: Urvashi had cursed Arjuna to lose his manhood, and Indra softened the curse to a single year of Arjuna's choosing, which became the year in hiding.

questions.q: How did the Pandavas keep people away from the tree where their weapons hung?
options: They posted Bhima to guard it through the nights
They paid the keepers of the burning ground to watch it
They hung charms of warning from its branches
They said the bundle was their aged mother's corpse, hung up by family custom
questions.explanation: Claiming the wrapped bundle was the corpse of their old mother, hung according to their family custom, they made the tree a place everyone avoided.

questions.q: Who led the first raid on Virata's cattle, striking from the south?
options: Susharma, king of the Trigartas
Shalya, king of Madra
Karna, king of Anga
Jayadratha, king of Sindhu
questions.explanation: Susharma of Trigarta, an old enemy of Matsya, attacked first and seized the southern herds.

questions.q: When King Virata was captured during the Trigarta raid, who rescued him?
options: Nakula, riding out on the king's horses
Prince Uttara with the palace guard
Bhima, fighting as the cook Vallabha
Sahadeva with an army of cowherds
questions.explanation: Bhima ran down Susharma's chariot, freed Virata, and took the Trigarta king captive in turn.

questions.q: When the Kauravas seized Virata's cattle in the north, who rode out against them?
options: King Virata with his full army
Prince Uttara with Brihannala driving his chariot
Kanka leading the palace garrison
The queen's kinsmen with their retainers
questions.explanation: With the army away in the south, the boastful prince Uttara rode out alone with the dance teacher Brihannala as his charioteer.

questions.q: Who arranged for Brihannala to drive prince Uttara's chariot?
options: Queen Sudeshna, who trusted the dance teacher
Kanka, hinting at a hidden warrior's skill
Prince Uttara himself, who admired the teacher
Sairandhri, who revealed that Brihannala had once been Arjuna's charioteer
questions.explanation: Sairandhri told the princess that Brihannala had driven Arjuna's chariot, and princess Uttara persuaded the teacher to take the reins.

questions.q: What did Arjuna do at the shami tree before facing the Kuru army?
options: Took down the Gandiva and revealed his true name to prince Uttara
Prayed to Shiva for the power of the Pashupatastra
Sent prince Uttara home to warn King Virata
Burned his Brihannala garments as an offering
questions.explanation: At the tree Arjuna retrieved his hidden weapons, strung the Gandiva, and told the terrified prince who he really was.

questions.q: How did Arjuna overcome the entire Kuru host single-handed at the cattle raid?
options: He challenged their champions to duels one by one
He scattered their chariots with the Pashupatastra
He used the Sammohana astra to put the army to sleep
He set their camp ablaze with arrows of fire
questions.explanation: Arjuna loosed the Sammohana astra, which stupefied the Kaurava warriors where they stood.

questions.q: What trophies did Arjuna gather from the defeated Kuru warriors?
options: Their banners, to hang in Virata's hall
Their fine garments, as playthings for princess Uttara's dolls
Their crowns, as tribute for King Virata
Their bows, to arm the soldiers of Matsya
questions.explanation: At the princess's earlier request, Arjuna had prince Uttara collect the rich garments of the stupefied warriors as dresses for her dolls.

questions.q: Who argued that the thirteen years were already complete when Arjuna revealed himself?
options: Drona, who refused to fight his favorite student
Vidura, pleading for peace before the court
Bhishma, reckoning the extra months of the lunar calendar
Krishna, arriving as an envoy from Dwarka
questions.explanation: Bhishma calculated that the intercalary months of the lunar calendar meant the full term had passed before Arjuna showed himself.

questions.q: How did Sairandhri explain her mysterious protection to curious questioners?
options: She claimed five Gandharva husbands watched over her
She claimed a sage's blessing shielded her from harm
She claimed to be under the queen's personal protection
She claimed the goddess Durga guarded her steps
questions.explanation: Draupadi said she was the wife of five Gandharvas who would destroy any man who wronged her.

questions.q: When Virata angrily struck Kanka and his nose bled, why was the blood caught in a vessel?
options: To hide the wound from the assembled court
To keep evidence of the king's offense
To tend the hurt without summoning physicians
So a righteous man's blood would not fall to the earth and blight the kingdom
questions.explanation: Sairandhri caught the blood because, as Yudhishthira later explained, had a righteous man's blood touched the ground, the king and his realm would have perished.

questions.q: Why did King Virata strike Kanka with a die during their game?
options: Kanka had won too many throws in a row
Kanka kept crediting Brihannala, not prince Uttara, with the victory over the Kurus
Kanka had hinted at the king's cowardice during the cattle raid
Kanka let slip that he had once served Yudhishthira
questions.explanation: Each time Virata praised his son's triumph, Kanka insisted Brihannala had won the battle, until the furious king flung a die at his face.

questions.q: Which book of the Mahabharata narrates the year the Pandavas spent in hiding?
options: Virata Parva
Vana Parva
Udyoga Parva
Sabha Parva
questions.explanation: The Virata Parva, the fourth book of the epic, is devoted entirely to the year of disguise.

questions.q: What news set the Kauravas planning the raid that nearly exposed the Pandavas?
options: A spy had glimpsed Sairandhri's unbound hair
A merchant reported five brahmins living strangely
Duryodhana dreamed of Bhima working in a kitchen
Kichaka had been slain by unseen Gandharvas, a feat fit only for Bhima
questions.explanation: Spies reported the mighty Kichaka crushed in the night by so-called Gandharvas, and the Kauravas reasoned only Bhima killed that way.

questions.q: Which earlier divine boon helped the Pandavas pass the year unrecognized?
options: Krishna's promise given at the dice hall
Durvasa's blessing won by their hospitality
The boon of Dharma, granted at the lake of the Yaksha
Indra's gift after the burning of Khandava
questions.explanation: After the riddles at the lake, Dharma had granted Yudhishthira that the brothers would not be recognized during the thirteenth year.

questions.q: What secret names did the brothers agree to use for one another in Virata's city?
options: Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala
Soma, Surya, Agni, Vayu, and Indra
Kanka, Vallabha, Brihannala, Granthika, and Tantipala
Dhira, Vira, Shura, Sthira, and Gambhira
questions.explanation: Beyond their public servant names, the five agreed on the call-names Jaya, Jayanta, Vijaya, Jayatsena, and Jayadbala for private use.

questions.q: Where did the Pandavas establish themselves immediately after the year of disguise ended?
options: At Indraprastha, their former capital
At Kampilya, the city of Drupada
At Upaplavya, a city of the Matsya kingdom
At Dwarka, as the guests of Krishna
questions.explanation: The Pandavas moved openly to Upaplavya in Matsya, where allies gathered and the war councils were held.

questions.q: What did prince Uttara do at his first sight of the assembled Kuru army?
options: He sounded his conch and charged in alone
He fled the chariot in terror until Brihannala chased him down
He fainted and had to be revived with river water
He tried to surrender the royal cattle at once
questions.explanation: The boastful prince leapt from the chariot and ran, and Brihannala had to chase him, calm him, and put him to the reins instead.

questions.q: What did Duryodhana claim when Arjuna's identity became known on the battlefield?
options: That Arjuna's celestial weapons had been used unfairly
That Virata had conspired to shelter fugitives
That Matsya's cattle were lawful Kuru property
That the Pandavas were found before the term ended and must repeat their exile
questions.explanation: Duryodhana insisted the discovery came within the thirteenth year, but Bhishma's calendar reckoning showed the term was already complete.
